title: kanbus_clock React runtime should not depend on Storybook
description:
Observed:
- kanbus_clock default React mode fetches frames from Storybook iframe
- runtime fails if Storybook server is not running

Expected:
- React rendering works without Storybook in runtime
- Storybook remains design-time only

Plan:
- add dedicated runtime entry/build for React components
- auto-serve runtime assets from kanbus_clock
- remove Storybook runtime flags from kanbus_clock

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Starting implementation: adding a dedicated React runtime bundle and switching kanbus_clock to serve that bundle locally instead of Storybook.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text:
Implemented runtime decoupling:
- Added dedicated React runtime bundle in storybook-app (`build-pixoo-runtime`) with a standalone runtime page (`runtime.html`) and entry (`src/runtime/main.tsx`).
- Refactored kanbus_clock React path to serve local runtime assets via an internal static server; removed Storybook runtime flags from CLI.
- Added fail-fast when runtime assets are missing with clear build command.
- Added tests for parser/runtime URL/asset checks/server lifecycle.

Validation:
- `python3 -m py_compile demos/kanbus_clock.py`
- `pytest tests/test_kanbus_clock.py -q` => 44 passed
- Manual smoke run shows `clock-source: react runtime http://127.0.0.1:<port>/runtime.html` with Storybook not running.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Validation complete and green. Waiting on live device acceptance: run kanbus_clock with React mode and Storybook stopped, confirm clock + notifications still render.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Adjusted runtime default clock face to the tuned Kanbus style: markerMode=dot12, no second hand, dim Radix bronze palette and geometry parity with PixooclockDefault. Rebuilt runtime bundle and restarted kanbus_clock for live verification.
